title: secula viscidula
intro: Secula viscidula, also known as the sticky thread moss or sticky thread fern, is a type of epiphytic plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Asia and is known for its sticky threads that help it to absorb moisture and nutrients from the air. Secula viscidula has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
